chunks.page_summary: This page provides a guide on accessing diagnostics and parameter readings for Rheonics PROFINET instruments using a master configuration tooling software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Diagnostics and Parameter Readings with Profinet

Modified on: Sun, 15 Feb, 2026 at 12:42 AM

The Rheonics PROFINET instruments offer many different modules and parameters. To select these modules/parameters, the master configuration tooling software is used.

1. Rheonics device provides 2 modules “Diagnostics_1” and “Device Parameters_1”.

Figure 1. Device overview Controller Address.
  - Software Interface Header:
    - Path: Profinet Integration with SMET ▸ Ungrouped devices ▸ rheonics-smet [Rheonics SMET]
    - View Tabs: Topology view, Network view, Device view
  - Device overview table:
    - Row 1 (rheonics-smet):
      - Module: rheonics-smet
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 0
      - Type: Rheonics SMET
      - Article no.: www.rheonics.com
      - Firmware: 1.0
    - Row 2 (X1):
      - Module: X1
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 0 X1
      - Type: rheonics-smet
    - Row 3 (X1 P1):
      - Module: X1 P1
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 0 X1 P1
      - Type: X1 P1
    - Row 4 (Diagnostics_1):
      - Module: Diagnostics_1
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 1
      - I address: 68...135
      - Type: Diagnostics
      - Firmware: 1.0
    - Row 5 (Device Parameters_1):
      - Module: Device Parameters_1
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 2
      - I address: 136...436
      - Type: Device Parameters
      - Firmware: 1.0
    - Row 6 (Empty):
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 3
    - Row 7 (Empty):
      - Rack: 0
      - Slot: 4
  - Device Parameters_1 [Device Parameters] lower pane:
    - Tabs: General, IO tags, System constants, Texts, Properties, Info, Diagnostics
    - System constants section (under 'Show hardware system constant ▼'):
      - Table columns: Name, Type, Hardware identi., Used by, Comment
      - Row 1:
        - Name: rheonics-smet~Device_Parameters_1
        - Type: Hw_SubModule
        - Hardware identi.: 277
        - Used by: PLC_1

2. Diagnostics_1
  1. This module covers data that is useful for the Rheonics Support team to help customers diagnose any issues.
key_facts: Rheonics PROFINET instruments provide various modules and parameters.
A master configuration tooling software is required to select PROFINET modules and parameters.
A Rheonics device offers two main modules: “Diagnostics_1” and “Device Parameters_1”.
The “Diagnostics_1” module provides data intended to assist the Rheonics Support team in diagnosing customer issues.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions and parameter tables for configuring diagnostic and process tags related to a Rheonics SMET sensor within a Siemens PLC environment using Profinet integration.
chunks.extracted_text:
3. Device Parameters_1

This module covers the 22 parameters available for the SMET, as well the sensor status parameter.

Figure 2. Diagnostic module.

key_facts: This module covers 22 parameters available for the SMET, including sensor status.
Tags in the PLC table are defined based on information from the GSD file for Profinet integration.
diagram_labels: Figure 3. Define tag in the plc table from gsd file.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays a software interface (TIA Portal and Rheonics Control Panel) used for monitoring real-time process parameters, including viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure, from a Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter.

Figure 4. Data monitor from SME showing the measured data in both the TIA portal and RCP.

For Variable mapping please refer to the table files on the Profinet GSD file for Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000401423).
key_facts: The document section describes how to go online and monitor parameters from a Rheonics device using a Siemens Totally Integrated Automation (TIA) Portal and the Rheonics Control Panel.
The Rheonics Control Panel S.4.0.0 displays real-time measured values for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure.
Detailed parameter data, including addresses and monitor values for viscosity, density, and temperature, are available in a parameter table within the TIA Portal.
A Profinet GSD file is available on the Rheonics support website for variable mapping of their inline viscometer and density meter.
